title: Guettarda antirhoea
intro: Guettarda antirhoea, also known as the "Red Trumpet Tree," is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Guettarda antirhoea plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Its bioactive constituents, which include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Guettarda antirhoea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Guettarda antirhoea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Guettarda antirhoea is a part of the genus Guettarda, which includes around 30 species of plants. The genus Guettarda is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Guettarda antirhoea is also known to be native to the Caribbean and parts of Central and South America.

benefits: Guettarda antirhoea is said to have potential health benefits, including antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage and inflammation.
